ddls:
CREATE TABLE "tweets" (
  "tweet_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each tweet */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the tweet was created */,
  "text" TEXT /* Text content of the tweet */,
  "retweet_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the tweet has been retweeted */,
  "retweeted" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the tweet has been retweeted */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who posted the tweet */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the source from which the tweet was collected */,
  "sentiment_score" REAL /* Sentiment score of the tweet (e.g., -1 to 1) */,
  "location" TEXT /* Geographical location from where the tweet was posted */,
  "language" TEXT /* Language of the tweet */,
  "hashtags" TEXT /* Comma-separated list of hashtags used in the tweet */,
  "mentions" TEXT /* Comma-separated list of user mentions in the tweet */,
  "media_url" TEXT /* URL to any media attached to the tweet (images, videos) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweets_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES sources ("source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweets_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., data analyst, admin, content manager) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the user account was created */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the last login */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the user (e.g., active, suspended) */,
  "organization_id" INTEGER /* ID of the organization the user belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role FOREIGN KEY ("role") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the source (e.g., Twitter, Facebook) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the source */,
  "api_endpoint" TEXT /* API endpoint used to fetch tweets from this source */,
  "last_synced" TEXT /* Date and time of the last successful data sync */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tweet_interactions" (
  "interaction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each interaction */,
  "tweet_id" TEXT /* ID of the tweet being interacted with */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user performing the interaction */,
  "interaction_type" TEXT /* Type of interaction (e.g., retweet, like, comment) */,
  "interaction_date" TEXT /* Date and time of the interaction */,
  "interaction_content" TEXT /* Content of the interaction (e.g., text of a comment) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("interaction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweet_interactions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweet_interactions_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sentiment_categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sentiment category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the sentiment category (e.g., Positive, Negative, Neutral) */,
  "score_range" TEXT /* Range of sentiment scores for the category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the sentiment category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., data analyst, admin, content manager) */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role (e.g., read, write, delete) */,
  "role_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)
